meaning: adj.冷淡的,不好客的,(地带,气候等)不适合居住的,(指地方)荒凉的
examples: Organizers collected a range of bold, sometimes zany, approaches to the threat of an increasingly inhospitable world.
That's probably the most bare, bleak, barren and inhospitable island I've ever seen.
It is home to the most inhospitable environment on the planet because it is supposed to be an extremely cold place.
New Yorkers are sometimes accused of being inhospitable to out-of-towners, but this is ridiculous.
Historically, the company has been known as an inhospitable place for small outside developers.
